senses.definition: the quality of being capable of reconciliation or bringing into harmony
examples: The reconciliability of the two conflicting plans is essential for a peaceful agreement.
Experts questioned the reconciliability of the law with current human rights standards.

senses.definition: able to be reconciled or brought into agreement or harmony
examples: The two sides found a reconciliable path to compromise.
Only a few points were reconciliable with the existing framework.

senses.definition: to restore friendly relations or harmony between people or groups
examples: The diplomat sought to reconciliate the two nations after years of tension.
Efforts were made to reconciliate the families after the dispute.

senses.definition: past tense of reconciliate; having restored friendly relations
examples: The leaders reconciliated after a long negotiation.
They reconciliated their differences and signed the agreement.

senses.definition: present participle of reconciliate; acting to restore harmony
examples: They are reconciliating the communities after the conflict.
Reconciliating the two departments requires careful mediation.

senses.definition: the act of bringing people or groups back into a friendly relationship; the process of reconciling
examples: Their reconciliation brought the family back together.
The reconciliation between the two countries took several years.

senses.definition: plural of reconciliation; processes or instances of restoring harmony
examples: The report documents several reconciliations between departments.
Different reconciliations were proposed during the talks.

senses.definition: related to reconciliation or intended to reconcile differences
examples: They proposed a reconciliatiory approach to the dispute.
The speech included several reconciliatiory suggestions.

senses.definition: tending to reconcile or make compatible; aimed at solving disagreements
examples: They adopted a conciliative policy to reduce tensions.
Her conciliative remarks softened the debate.

senses.definition: a person who seeks to reconcile or mediate between conflicting parties
examples: The reconciliator helped both sides reach a settlement.
As a reconciliator, he often works behind the scenes.

senses.definition: tending to reconcile; aimed at making peace
examples: The speech contained conciliatory language.
They took a conciliatory stance to avoid further conflict.

senses.definition: present participle of reconcile; to restore friendly relations or agreement
examples: They are reconciling their accounts for the quarterly report.
She is reconciling the family's differences.

senses.definition: in a manner that seeks to reconcile or pacify
examples: He spoke reconcilingly to soothe tensions.
The diplomats moved reconcilingly toward a compromise.

senses.definition: to conclude again or anew; to reach a final conclusion after reconsideration
examples: The panel may reconclude their findings after new evidence.
We will reconclude the committee’s report tomorrow.

senses.definition: a second or renewed conclusion; a conclusion reached after reconsideration
examples: The report ends with a reconclusion that differs from the initial draft.
There was a reconclusion after new data emerged.

senses.definition: to make or devise something again, often by using whatever is available; to create or invent again, sometimes in a faulty way
examples: She managed to reconcoct a decent story from thin air.
They reconcocted a plan after their first idea failed.

senses.definition: to make concrete again; to render something more tangible or definite
examples: We must reconcretize the project goals before approval.
The idea was reconcreted into a clear plan.

senses.definition: to occur again at the same time; to coincide or happen together
examples: These events reconcur every year.
The two phenomena reconcur during winter months.

senses.definition: hidden, concealed; covered by a layer, especially of condensation
examples: The recond surface of the glass remained misty.
Hidden recond details emerged later in the report.

senses.definition: to condemn again or more strongly; to pronounce a renewed or intensified judgment against
examples: They recondemned the policy after new evidence.
The council recondemned the act as dangerous and unlawful.

senses.definition: the act of condemning again or renewed condemnation
examples: The report includes a recondemnation of the prior policy.
Public recondemnation followed the incident.

senses.definition: the process of condensation again; condensation occurring anew
examples: The gas undergoes recondensation as it cools.
Recondensation was observed in the experimental setup.

senses.definition: plural of recondensation; multiple instances of condensation again
examples: Various recondensations occur under the test conditions.
Different recondensations were noted in the results.

senses.definition: to condense again; to change from a gaseous to a liquid state again
examples: We will recondense the vapor to liquid.
The substance began to recondense as it cooled.

senses.definition: condensed again; previously condensed and then condensed again
examples: The recondensed gas formed droplets on the surface.
A recondensed layer appeared after cooling.
